body_txt: When a character gives a name to their personal weapon. Some people name their arms . These people name their "arms." Why? They have a sentimental attachment to said weapon . They are a veteran from some war or a major Gun Nut . The name once belonged to someone they loved . They have a tenuous grasp on reality . Maybe it's several of the above. In any case, someone who not only brandishes a knife but tells you 'her' name is Breanne is generally not one to mess with. A subtrope of Named Possession and Named Weapons — this trope only applies when the name is a nickname given by the wielder, not the make or model of the weapon or an otherwise "official" name. However, as the character's legend grows, "Vera" might become famous as well. This occasionally gets invoked, where a craftsman or athlete names his favorite tool(s) as a way of indicating the character's skill or passion. Sister trope to Cute as a Bouncing Betty (when a type of weapon is given a cute name), Legendary Weapon (for when the name and the weapon become truly famous), and Stock Weapon Names (for when common names are used between works). The inverse is They Call Him "Sword" , which occurs when somebody gets nicknamed for a weapon. Empathic Weapons and Evil Weapons are a different issue. Ships and planes nearly always have a name. I Call Him "Mister Happy" is a different named "weapon". No relation to And Call Him "George" . Can overlap with Cargo Ship (but try not to think about it too hard).
